name: Elbmesh Explain Action Event Flow
title: Explain an Elbmesh Action/Event Flow
summary: Explain what happens from an Elbmesh Action or Event through Resources, Policies, Events, Reactions, External Operations, Views, Queries, journals, and execution trace gaps.
description: Use this task to produce an implementation-aware flow explanation without overstating future Elbmesh CLI, generated docs, NATS, Restate, or Operation Journal behavior.

outputs.title: Current implemented versus target architecture notes

validations.title: Flow explainability review
criteria: The explanation preserves Action -> Event -> Reaction -> Action boundaries.
The explanation identifies Views and Queries as derived read-side outputs rather than replay state.
The explanation states current implemented code/tests separately from target architecture for CLI, NATS, Restate, generated docs, and Operation Journal behavior.

items: Inspect manifest definitions, docs, PPP tasks, and source/tests for the named Action/Event/Reaction/View/Query.
Identify the target Resource and any Policies that govern the Action when known.
List Events that can be recorded and which Resource stream owns them.
Traverse Reactions as Action -> Event -> Reaction -> Action edges only.
Identify declared External Operations and state whether operation idempotency is currently implemented, tested, or only target architecture.
Identify Views updated by Projection code and declared Queries or ViewStore primitives that expose results.
Describe Action Journal, Reaction Journal, Operation Journal, and execution trace entries/gaps relevant to success, rejection, runtime failure, and incomplete outcomes.

criteria.text: The explanation uses Elbmesh terminology accurately: Resource, Action, Event, Reaction, External Operation, View, Query, Policy, Action Journal, Operation Journal, Reaction Journal, execution trace, and manifest.
Future architecture such as Operation Journal, Restate, NATS adapters, generated docs, and explain-flow CLI is not overstated as current implementation.
Claims are backed by source docs, PPP tasks, Rust code, tests, or explicit missing-implementation notes.

blocks.description: Target explain-flow command and core rules.
Trace entries, outcomes, gaps, overlays, and visualization targets.
